title: Export monday workflow drawing to a PDF / JPG / excel / word file
excerpt:
Hello all ! 
I’d like to Export a monday workflow drawing to a PDF / JPG / excel / word file, in order to create a local documentation. 
Is it possible to do this kind export file ? 
tks!

posts.author: andreastu (andrea stuginski)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello all !

I’d like to Export a monday workflow drawing to a PDF / JPG / excel / word file, in order to create a local documentation.

Is it possible to do this kind export file ?
tks!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: To confirm, do you mean a workflow or a workcanvas?

posts.author: arjumehta (Arju)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Dear,

Open your workflow board and click the three-dot menu. Choose Export to download as PDF or Excel. For JPG or Word, export as PDF first, then convert using tools like Adobe Acrobat.

posts.author: andreastu (andrea stuginski)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Arju, tks for your help ! I’ve already tried this path but this option does not appear for me. I uploaded the image of my board, just in case I’m doing something wrong

workflow options1239×769 74 KB

posts.author: andreastu (andrea stuginski)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Desiree, it is the workflow. Tks

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
sunilkumar:

Export PDF/JPG/Word file

Currently, you cannot export your workflow as a PDF, JPG, or Word file directly from this interface. The workflow builder is focused on creating and managing automations within monday.com, and does not include a built-in export feature for visual or document formats.

If you need a visual or document version of your workflow, you can take a screenshot of your workflow canvas and save it as an image, or use third-party tools to convert screenshots to PDF or Word documents.
